speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's late afternoon, I believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Clearly, we're going to talk to El Jefe. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Got it out of the way right away. Autocorrect. Inside story. Inside joke. Whatever.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The inside story.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I think we should go talk to him. That seems like it would be smart, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and we already signed one contract with him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's not like he wouldn't know where we went if we left, anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What time of day is it right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If I recall, it's later afternoon.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That is my voice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm dialing it in still. (vocalizes trying to find Nott's voice)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And we got there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A little more mid, a little less treble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You make your way across the Pentamarket, to the familiar down, dank, and broody interior of the Evening Nip tavern. You pass by Clive, who gives you a nod. You head towards the hidden staircase down to the subterranean tavern. There a different sound catches your ears. There's the familiar sound of warm music and tavern flair, but you also hear rowdy shouts and cheers and the occasional sound of something heavy against a skidding table. As you slowly descend toward the base of the curved staircase to enter the main chamber, you can see some folks are sitting at the tables, The Gentleman is in his far corner at the back, and they're all watching a small circle of people in the center as two individuals in the process of beating the shit out of each other. Bare fists, bare knuckled. People are making bets, laughing and cheering, and throwing beer and various objects into the ring to mess with them. It is mid-craze scuffle at the moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And we got there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A little more mid, a little less treble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's late afternoon, I believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can head over.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I get nervous spending too much time in one place, but if everyone's going, then we're going.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's like a step in the way out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if what he wants us to do means that we have to stay here for a while, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Then we'll have to make it worth our while.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like for a lot of money, would you stay?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're at a table, you're doing well. Eventually, you're going to want to walk away from the table.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if the table offers you four times the amount you've ever seen?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The table always offers you four times the amount you've ever seen. You got to walk away while you're winning. But all right!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we'll make a decision.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Getting a glimpse here as you watch a bit of parting between shoulders as everyone's standing end to end. You hear this heavy crack and the two people nearest to you bow backward. Apparently, one of the two fighting individuals being thrusted into the circle is immediately pushed back into the center. You hear another couple of swings go before they both breakaway. You can hear the heavy breaths of them now exhausted, pouring with sweat. You recognize them, one is Louis, one of the agents who fled from the research facility that you were then going after, and the other is Thed, the halfling fellow with the neck tattoo that you saved in the sewers not but a week and half earlier. They are both bloodied--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's a fight club! You guys, it's a fucking fight club!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Beau is in her element.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys step down into the main area, the large ogre bodyguard stands and turns back. His low guttural grunt come through his protruding jawline with a (grunt) and gives you a sign to continue inward.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How have you been, Kutha?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, me too, all right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That's an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's totally fine. I did it to myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sitting on one of the table edges, you see Dweez, the little creepy gnome who you met earlier, who's like, (breathy laugh).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't like him. That was creepy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, a familiar voice kicks up from the back corner, "Friends, come sit. Thank you for such a fine display of sportsmanship."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's us. Always down for a scrap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Apparently." The Gentleman now sits there. His comfortable attire today comes in a billowing linen, a dark navy blue shirt that frills at the wrists. You can see his rings and jewelry available as his fingers are crossed and partially stapled-- steepled in front of him. Not stapled to him. That's a whole different thing. Ah! Oh god!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use this time talking to him as a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You spend an hour here, and you'll be fine. "Thank you for coming so quickly."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's totally fine. I did it to myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sitting on one of the table edges, you see Dweez, the little creepy gnome who you met earlier, who's like, (breathy laugh).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't like him. That was creepy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, a familiar voice kicks up from the back corner, "Friends, come sit. Thank you for such a fine display of sportsmanship."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's us. Always down for a scrap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Apparently." The Gentleman now sits there. His comfortable attire today comes in a billowing linen, a dark navy blue shirt that frills at the wrists. You can see his rings and jewelry available as his fingers are crossed and partially stapled-- steepled in front of him. Not stapled to him. That's a whole different thing. Ah! Oh god!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use this time talking to him as a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, hopefully--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Those are the two at the moment. If those don't tickle your fancy, I'm sure we could have another opportunity in the next week or two. I just would hate to see these go to less qualified individuals."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Leading to Berleben.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could you describe your friend, Vabron?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Febron Keyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Febron Keyes, (chuckles) he's memorable. Based by his fairly emanate lack of teeth. His one wandering left eye. A bit of a skin condition."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No grill, busted eye. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I just drooled a little. Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "But he's good at the work he does."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What work is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Keeping his mouth shut and knowing where the secrets are and not giving those secrets up. He's been pressed for it a few times and has proven to be trustworthy, and I admire that."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Would we have the same sort of timetable for this mission, a month?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The sooner the better, as the longer these shipments continues to elude us, the more money we're losing. Like I said, a timely manner, there may be a bonus."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Anything else?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You don't get an exhaustion point, you just become stunned for a round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hard to keep track of parties outside of our purview.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am over Zadash.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just over it, huh? Plus, it'll give us time to leave? Maybe things will change by the time we come back? Or maybe not. We'll see. Never know what happens outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we're going to take them all? Hop on both of his offers, I think?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, quite frankly, I am a little more comfortable in Quannah Breach anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're more nomadic?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it, yeah. We'll pound our drinks; keep them away from Beau because she'll drink all of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I just want to make sure we're here for a full hour, though. I'm going to drink slowly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can use a little-- yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to sip it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The exhaustion point lasts until you take a long rest.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No. It just stuns you for one round. Which in combat is really bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we're going to take them all? Hop on both of his offers, I think?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, quite frankly, I am a little more comfortable in Quannah Breach anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're more nomadic?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it, yeah. We'll pound our drinks; keep them away from Beau because she'll drink all of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I just want to make sure we're here for a full hour, though. I'm going to drink slowly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can use a little-- yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to sip it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The exhaustion point lasts until you take a long rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't get an exhaustion point, you just become stunned for a round.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: For a round, it's gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: For the Haste?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You're fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just a pain in the ass in combat, not in out of combat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Once it wears off you're like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I thought you get a point of exhaustion after Haste.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Dweez comes up from behind a moment later with a stack of regular parchment and ink. He hands it over. It does not look like it would be the necessary enchanted or prepared inks that you could use to scribe spells per se, but it's parchment and ink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, I would not ensure such a mission to him." He glances past you, in the far corner you can see him now leaning over the bar, two full cups in, starting to get a little dazed on the stool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Louis! Fuck you!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: His hand does this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep, he's a winner.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You will get 107 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anything else you require?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. This has really whet our appetite.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Swamp pun?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I just can't stop staring at his face, it's soaking wet! Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anyway, if our business is complete. I appreciate your patronage, I look forward to our continued partnership, and I wish you all the luck."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't sweat the small stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fucking shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb hangs back all by himself and says (stuttering) Paper and ink?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You're looking at about a 75-mile journey. With the cart and the horses, you're looking at about 30 miles a day at a normal pace. You're probably looking at two nights' stay on the road before you reach your destination.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think we'll go at a regular pace until we get to the Bromkiln Byway. At the Amber Road, we should be all right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys push forward and watch as, behind you, the large, looming walls and towers and buildings of Zadash that had been your home for weeks disappear slowly behind you. Once again, you're embraced by the wilds and open landscapes of the valley. You take a moment to reflect upon the insanity and craziness of the urban atmosphere and the peace, as well as the unknown, that comes with traversing such an open plain. I'd say you have maybe a few hours before the sun sets. Right as you hit the intersection between the Amber Road and the Bromkiln Byway, the sun has set. The sky is dark; the clouds prevent starlight from coming through, and what moonlight would be visible is choked, giving you only the faint glimmer of the moon's location behind the cloud cover. What would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think we'll go at a regular pace until we get to the Bromkiln Byway. At the Amber Road, we should be all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're looking at about a 75-mile journey. With the cart and the horses, you're looking at about 30 miles a day at a normal pace. You're probably looking at two nights' stay on the road before you reach your destination.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As the night has fallen and you spend the better part of the next hour trying to look for any physical representation of safety or cover, it is just open rolling hills and field. There might be places where you could stay low and perhaps keep relatively safe, but nothing that sticks out to you, unfortunately. What do you plan to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's nighttime now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I bet someone's going to try to jump out at us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We should not let them do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They do that to people that are on the open road.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why would you tell me that? Now I'm very scared!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are there any tree clusters or rock clusters or boulders or anything that provides shelter?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At your current location? Make a perception check with disadvantage because it is nighttime.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm the wrong person to be doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was going to say I was hoping to find the same thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I put on the goggles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you can both make individual rolls, or one of you can aid the other.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll aid you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take your advantage on your roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nope. That was a two with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What did you roll, a one and a two?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A two and a one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's really shit.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys get part way through your watch, conversing, muttering about the recent events, the sun comes up, uneventful.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if you're going to be that impressed by it, but maybe you'll like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You can see your breath (exhales).

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Well, I wonder, I mean, I wonder if anyone else-- I think, is our time up, or--?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think so. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That was such church youth trip flirting!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great first date.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With that, your watch comes uneventful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: God's really cool, right? Right, right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Third and final watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me and Caleb. Me and Molly, I guess. I'm on third watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take third watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nott and Molly. Who's helming this one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What's your-- your perception's probably high as balls.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You don't know!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Advantage on yours. Actually, it would have been the first one. I started to realize, it's advantage, but disadvantage because it's night.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, ten. Why is it disadvantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Because it's night.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Continuing onward, about ten minutes later you get just around the edge of where that hill was, and you can see the source of the smoke. There, nestled between a small cluster of trees and a bit of natural rock formations, a small shack that the smoke is coming from.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is it into the woods, or further along?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You cannot see. It's over one of the larger hills that's blocking what its source is. It's about a mile or so off of the road if you were to go ahead in that direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's about a mile off the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's just chimney smoke. Just keep moving.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't look like chimney smoke. I'm not sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So there's fire. So what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Campfire smoke?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like campfire smoke?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It could be. It could be chimney smoke. It's not a billowing column.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's not a billowing column.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: When you say it, it has such a different color to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's people, though. Or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're burning people?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I mean, just be on alert. They could be burning anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We call that a barbecue. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll find, once and for all, the answer: What do children taste like?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys continue onward for the rest of the day until, eventually, the sun sets and you find yourselves with evening quickly taking the sky.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Meat and--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nah.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, okay. Shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys mount back up on your horses and make your way back to the rest of the group on the cart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What kind of armor is it out of?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's hide armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's hide armor?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's well-made, hand-crafted hide armor, for anybody who would have use for it, whether it be using or reselling it. That's up to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nothing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We made a friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you kill someone for that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: He's dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He's very dead. And then he rose up from the grave again and we had to kill him again. Twice. Same man.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: But that shack is now ours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Great!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great! Can we stay the night there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we should just live here instead of going anywhere else.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking of planting soybeans.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is our kingdom.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: This is our keep. What do we name it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Small Shit Keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's keep going.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You glance off, and maybe about 30 minutes up the road you can see a small forest, maybe two or three miles in diameter. It's a cluster of trees and would probably be a way to camp off of the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: But that shack is now ours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Great!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great! Can we stay the night there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we should just live here instead of going anywhere else.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking of planting soybeans.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is our kingdom.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: This is our keep. What do we name it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Small Shit Keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's keep going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys continue onward for the rest of the day until, eventually, the sun sets and you find yourselves with evening quickly taking the sky.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's make camp before we completely lose light this time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Good call.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Who's helming the search for a good place to camp?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll look this time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll help.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perception check with advantage because the sun is still up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Don't fuck this up, Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You rolled the same, well, almost the same.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, but I'm helping him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So he got advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Plus? But you said advantage? Oh, right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, you can do that. You manage to set up right as the sun completes setting. You build up this little campfire in the center. You set up your bedding around it. The cart is a little ways off. Unless you're putting the bedding in the cart and putting that there next to it? Okay. You put the cart there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's good. It's a little dry, but it's salted. It's cured. It'll keep for a while. A little chewy.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's very good. From tasting it, do I know what it is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If only I had help counting to the number four!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever tried human before?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: We'll see. Don't fuck me, Gil. Five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's very good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think I'm going to want more of this. I'm not sure where it's coming from, but I definitely think--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I like it a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm getting a taste for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's a delicacy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to start making a nice, pretty bed in the cart. It's going to be really nice looking.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: For all of us or just you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: For anyone who wants to stay in here with me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I would like that. LAURA Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to make a fire again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can break off some branches and some kindling.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's trees.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you a little while. You start getting a little nervous, because Yasha just vanishes in the darkness, saying, "I'll be right back," and is gone for like 40 minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll a normal roll because you are keeping an eye through darkness.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But I have night vision?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Night vision is only out to a certain radius.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You both maintain the first watch as you continue looking out towards the darkness beyond the visible trees that are refracting what little bit of light makes it to their bark. Looking into the shadows, your watch comes to an end, uneventful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm so glad to be out of that bloody city.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, me too, I guess. I just am excited to see more, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's nice to be on the road again.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's nice to be on the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've never been up this way before.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You haven't?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Not that I can remember. Not that I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's true. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: If I look around, are there flowers anywhere?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You haven't done that before.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Where's investigation? Oh, eight.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Looking this way, as he nudged you, you see the wolves, these goblin creatures here, and this large, looming ogre that was shouting across the way to the other ogre, who's now pushing past the tree and coming into view.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm not cursing!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, Fjord, Molly.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's really what I do best, guys. It's my most consistent thing that I do is roll shit initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Through both campaigns.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: In life and in-- Yeah, always.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15 to ten?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Wow. Ten to five?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Six!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh wait, I never said mine. 15. Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I was expecting another six.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what'd you have Molly?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I had 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Jester and Yasha at the same time. Top of the combat round, after the arrows were fired and Caleb is wincing and pulling back from the blow. As these creatures begin to come out of the shadows, preparing for their continuation of their assault, Beau, you're up first.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh Jesus. Oh boy. Where am I?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're right there. Caleb nudged you awake.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I wake up and I go  Fight Club! I run towards whatever I see first. Do I see the wolf? Do I see the goblins?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Nice, 18 points of damage on it. That finishes Beau's turn. It is now the goblins' go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You stay away, you piece of--! It's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you pull back and look, the wolf is frozen solid, and tumbles into a bunch of ice chunks. Off the battlefield. That ends the wolf's turn. Top of the round: Beau, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to unleash on this ogre in front of me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have the ogre and the wolf, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll do my first two attacks on the ogre. Natural 18 on the first one, and the second one sucks at a four, with a ten total?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That misses. The first one hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Goddamn it, six damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll go ahead and do a Flurry of Blows for my bonus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you have two more strikes. Against the ogre as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's unleash into the ogre. Okay, 19?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 19 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And 14?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14 also hits. Roll damage on both of those. d4s on those.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, ten damage on the first, eight on the second.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That ends Beau's go. It's now the goblins' turn. Yeah. They're going to flee. This one's just running.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (whacking) (snarl) You actually jam it in part of the giant wound. Part of its muscle is flopping over from where Yasha had struck him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hit under his arm! Hit his ribs like you did with me! Hit his ribs!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hit his ribs? Okay! Hearing Jester, I'm going to do Flurry of Blows, which I wasn't going to do, but hearing Jester, I'm going to because I felt so fucking inspired. I'm going to, leaning off of the ogre, do like a (pop pop pop pop) into his ribs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Two strikes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You almost shattered that screen!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I did. First one is 19, second one is 13.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay! Fucking want to (mumbling). Die! Okay, first one is a seven, second one is a six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Seven and six. Nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (yelling) Ah, damn it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're pummeling its torso, and its belly is (jiggling).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: As I'm punching, I'm going  Die! Die!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, it's middle of the night, maybe one or two in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Short bows, scimitars, grungy, some of them coated with excrement and other things to make them nasty when they carve through and infect wounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What about the big old weapons that the two bruisers were-- are they massive?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Large greatclubs that are carved.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Did he say excrement?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On some of the scimitars. They look nasty.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are they wearing underwear?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (horrified yelling)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But there are a few pouches that haven't been spilled or utilized entirely of toxin.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Of toxin? Can I grab those?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With that investigation check, you manage to pull five vials of toxin, unused. Each one has a single use on some sort of a weapon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Five vials of toxin.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I've got them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to draw that in my journal.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm going to rejoin the group.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, not that that wasn't terribly exciting. Still crazy dark out?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He was too busy with his own. Anyway, thank you guys. We'll pick this up next week on Thursday from there, as we have about a day's worth of travel before you get there. With enough speed, you will get there before the next dusk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Five more hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just live here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If I didn't have anything to do tomorrow. If I wasn't flying to Canada tomorrow, I could keep going.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh no, you have a thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did I miss that in the announcements?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I think you did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You just don't pay attention.
